proven.claim: Proven: "derive" appears in help (line 542) AND is implemented as
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 542
; Proven: "derive" appears in help (line 542) AND is implemented as
; case "derive": await runDerive(rest) in the switch.
(declare-const derive_documented Int)
(declare-const derive_implemented Int)
(assert (= derive_documented 1))
(assert (= derive_implemented 1))
(assert (not (= derive_documented derive_implemented)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: unsat — derive is both documented and implemented

violations.claim: Reachable violation: the relative order of "derive" and "verify" is INVERTED
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 542
; Reachable violation: the relative order of "derive" and "verify" is INVERTED
; between the switch statement and the help text.
; Switch order (by case appearance): analyze(2) → verify(3) → derive(4)
; Help order (by line appearance): analyze(541) → derive(542) → verify(543)
; This inconsistency means the implicit priority/grouping signaled by ordering differs.
(declare-const verify_switch_pos Int)
(declare-const derive_switch_pos  Int)
(declare-const verify_help_pos    Int)
(declare-const derive_help_pos    Int)
; Switch: case "verify" is the 3rd case, case "derive" is the 4th
(assert (= verify_switch_pos 3))
(assert (= derive_switch_pos  4))
; Help: derive is on line 542, verify is on line 543
(assert (= derive_help_pos  542))
(assert (= verify_help_pos  543))
; Transition (switch): verify comes BEFORE derive (verify_pos < derive_pos)
(assert (< verify_switch_pos derive_switch_pos))
; Transition (help): derive comes BEFORE verify (derive_pos < verify_pos)
(assert (< derive_help_pos verify_help_pos))
; Is the ordering inconsistent? (relative order differs in switch vs help)
(assert (and (< verify_switch_pos derive_switch_pos)
             (< derive_help_pos   verify_help_pos)))
(check-sat)
; Expected: sat — verify precedes derive in the switch but follows it in help;
; the two orderings are mutually inconsistent
